metadata.title: TB to KB converter – convert TB to KB
metadata.description: Convert terabytes to kilobytes and back. 1 TB is 1,073,741,824 KB in the binary system. Formula, Excel tip and table. Runs in your browser with no sign-up.

hero.title: Convert terabytes to kilobytes
hero.description: Type a number of terabytes and read the result in kilobytes straight away. A terabyte is 1,073,741,824 kilobytes in the binary system (1,024 × 1,024 × 1,024), so the converter multiplies your value by 1,073,741,824. Use Swap order to convert kilobytes to terabytes instead.

software.name: Terabytes to kilobytes converter – TB to KB calculator
alternateName: How many KB in a TB
software.description: Terabytes to kilobytes converter that runs in your browser with no sign-up. Type a number of terabytes and read the result in kilobytes instantly. It uses the binary system, where 1 TB is 1,073,741,824 KB.
featureList: Converts terabytes to kilobytes and kilobytes to terabytes in the binary system (1 TB = 1,073,741,824 KB)
The conversion works both ways — the Swap order button switches the direction
The result appears instantly as you type, with no button to click
The Copy result button puts the finished value on your clipboard
Standard toggle: compute in binary (1,024, like Windows and RAM) or decimal (1,000, like drives, macOS and search engines)
Gives a ready-made formula for Excel and spreadsheets
Every calculation runs locally in your browser, so your data never leaves your device
The exact formula is: kilobytes = terabytes × 1,073,741,824
Works on phone, tablet and computer with no installation

howTo.name: How do you convert terabytes to kilobytes?
howTo.description: A short guide to using the terabytes to kilobytes converter.

steps.name: Type a number of terabytes
steps.text: In the left field, type the number of terabytes you want to convert to kilobytes. You can use a dot or a comma as the decimal separator.

steps.name: Read the result in kilobytes
steps.text: The result in kilobytes appears immediately in the field on the right. The Copy result button puts it on your clipboard.

steps.name: Swap the direction if you need to
steps.text: Click Swap order to move to converting kilobytes to terabytes.

contentBlocks.title: Why convert terabytes to kilobytes?
contentBlocks.html:
A terabyte is enormous next to a kilobyte: 1 TB is 1,073,741,824 KB. You rarely need this conversion, but it comes up when a script, report or spec expects a value in kilobytes while you know the capacity in terabytes.

The relationship is fixed: 1 TB is 1,073,741,824 KB in the binary system, which operating systems and this converter use. To turn terabytes into kilobytes, multiply their number by 1,073,741,824. The converter does this the moment you type a value.

Where does that number come from? Because there are three steps of 1,024 between the terabyte and the kilobyte: TB → GB → MB → KB. 1,024 × 1,024 × 1,024 is 1,073,741,824. Below you will find a ready-made Excel formula and a table of the whole unit ladder.

This tool computes both standards: binary (1,024) by default, and the Unit standard button switches it to decimal (1,000). Windows and RAM count in binary, while drive makers, macOS and search engines count in decimal — which is why the same capacity is quoted two different ways.
contentBlocks.imageAlt: Free terabytes to kilobytes converter – Arteon

contentBlocks.title: How does the terabytes to kilobytes conversion work? Formula and example
contentBlocks.html:
The conversion rests on a fixed relationship: one terabyte is 1,073,741,824 kilobytes. To turn terabytes into kilobytes, multiply their number by 1,073,741,824. You can also multiply by 1,024 three times — from TB to GB, then to MB, then to KB.

Full formula: kilobytes = terabytes × 1,073,741,824. The other way: terabytes = kilobytes ÷ 1,073,741,824.

Example: you have 2 TB. Calculation: 2 × 1,073,741,824 = 2,147,483,648 KB. The numbers grow fast, because each terabyte is over a billion kilobytes.

In Excel, type the formula =A1*1073741824 in a cell, where A1 holds the value in TB. The other way, kilobytes to terabytes, use =A1/1073741824.

contentBlocks.title: Terabytes to kilobytes conversion table
contentBlocks.description: The table below converts terabytes into kilobytes in the binary system (×1,073,741,824). To convert another value, use the converter above.
contentBlocks.caption: Example: 1 TB × 1,073,741,824 = 1,073,741,824 KB. Want to convert kilobytes to terabytes? Click Swap order in the converter above.

contentBlocks.title: The full data unit ladder: from smallest to largest
contentBlocks.description: Every data size unit grows by the same multiplier: each one is 1,024 times larger than the previous. The table below shows the order from byte to petabyte, with a typical example of what each unit describes.

rows.label: Megabyte (MB)
values: a photo or an MP3 song

rows.label: Gigabyte (GB)
values: a movie or a RAM stick

contentBlocks.caption: Order from smallest to largest: byte, kilobyte, megabyte, gigabyte, terabyte, petabyte. Each step is a multiplication by 1,024.

contentBlocks.title: When do people most often convert terabytes to kilobytes?
contentBlocks.description: The jump from terabytes to kilobytes comes up mostly in technical situations.

items.title: Values in KB in systems and scripts
items.description: Some scripts, APIs or config fields expect a size in kilobytes. When you know the capacity in terabytes, you convert it to KB — 1 TB is 1,073,741,824 KB.

items.title: Grasping the scale
items.description: Converting shows just how large a number of kilobytes hides behind a terabyte. 2 TB is 2,147,483,648 KB, which makes the gap between the units clear.

items.title: A formula in Excel
items.description: In a spreadsheet you convert a whole column of capacities with =A1*1073741824. The converter gives the ready-made formula, so you do not have to look it up or remember it.

items.title: The order of the units
items.description: The converter and the ladder table show the order plainly, from smallest to largest: B, KB, MB, GB, TB, PB, each 1,024 times larger than the one before.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about converting terabytes to kilobytes

items.question: How many KB are in 1 TB?
items.answer: 1 TB is 1,073,741,824 KB in the binary system, which this converter and operating systems use. That is 1,024 × 1,024 × 1,024. In the decimal system (SI), 1 TB is 1,000,000,000 KB.

items.question: How do you convert terabytes to kilobytes?
items.answer: Multiply the number of terabytes by 1,073,741,824. You can also multiply by 1,024 three times: from TB to GB, then to MB, then to KB. The converter does this the moment you type a value.

items.question: How do you convert TB to KB in Excel?
items.answer: Type the formula =A1*1073741824 in a cell, where A1 holds the value in terabytes. The other way, kilobytes to terabytes, use =A1/1073741824. The same formula works in Google Sheets.

items.question: What is the order of the units from smallest to largest?
items.answer: From smallest: byte (B), kilobyte (KB), megabyte (MB), gigabyte (GB), terabyte (TB), petabyte (PB). Each unit is 1,024 times larger than the previous one. A byte describes a single character, while a terabyte describes a drive capacity.

items.question: How do you convert kilobytes to terabytes?
items.answer: Divide the number of kilobytes by 1,073,741,824. For example, 1,073,741,824 KB ÷ 1,073,741,824 = 1 TB. Click Swap order to have the converter work in this direction.

items.question: Why 1,024 and not 1,000?
items.answer: Because computers count in binary. Operating systems and this converter use the binary system, where each step is 1,024. Storage makers use the decimal system (1,000), which is why a drive's capacity shown in the system is smaller than the number on the label.

contentBlocks.title: Have a question or suggestion?
contentBlocks.description: If you have spotted an error in the table or have an idea to improve the converter, write to us. We reply to every message.
